title: Fix optimizer state reset bug in neural-network.ts
description: SGD/Adam/RMSProp optimizers reinitialize velocity/moment buffers when called with different-length arrays. train() at neural-network.ts:330-347 calls optimizer.update() alternating between flattened weights and biases of varying sizes per layer, triggering the length check at optimizers.ts:39,94,151. Adam and RMSProp degrade to vanilla SGD. Fix by creating per-parameter-group optimizer instances or making optimizers use a keyed state map.
acceptanceCriteria: Adam optimizer maintains moment buffers across weight and bias updates within same layer
Training with Adam converges faster than SGD on XOR dataset (verified by test)
Existing optimizer unit tests still pass

title: Wire orphaned visualization components into module
description: 5 files in src/components/ml-design/ (CNNVisualizer.tsx:642 lines, NeuralNetViz.tsx:505, TrainingDashboard.tsx:517, LossLandscapeCanvas.tsx:438, DecisionBoundaryCanvas.tsx:226) are not imported anywhere. These include 3D isometric CNN architecture viz, interactive training dashboard with optimizer selection, and 3D loss landscape renderer. Wire them into the module as advanced mode options or sub-panels.
acceptanceCriteria: All 5 orphaned components are reachable via the module UI
CNNVisualizer accessible from NN mode or a dedicated CNN mode
LossLandscapeCanvas accessible from NN mode after training
No regression in existing 6 modes

title: Cancel training RAF loop on mode switch
description: runBatch() at MLDesignModule.tsx:3525-3548 uses requestAnimationFrame loop that continues when user switches away from neural-network mode. The loop checks cancelRef.current but this is only set on unmount (line 3556), not on mode change. Add cancelRef.current = true when mode changes away from neural-network.
acceptanceCriteria: Switching from neural-network to another mode mid-training stops the RAF loop within 1 frame
Switching back to neural-network shows the last training state (not cleared)
No console errors on mode switch during training

title: Split MLDesignModule.tsx into mode-specific files
description: MLDesignModule.tsx is 3,737 lines containing 25+ components for 6 modes. Split into: modes/nn/ (NNSidebar, NNCanvas, NNProperties, NNBottomPanel, CNNDemoPanel, DropoutDemoPanel), modes/pipeline/, modes/serving/, modes/ab-testing/, modes/bandit/, modes/feature-store/. Keep useMLDesignModule hook in the root file as orchestrator.
acceptanceCriteria: No single file exceeds 600 lines
All 6 modes render identically before and after split
TypeScript compiles with no new errors
Each mode directory has sidebar, canvas, properties, and bottom-panel components

title: Add comprehensive ARIA accessibility to all interactive elements
description: Entire module has zero ARIA attributes. Add: role='tab' + aria-selected to mode tabs (line 966), role='option' + aria-label to dataset/activation buttons, role='img' + aria-label to all SVG visualizations, tabIndex + onKeyDown to pipeline stage <g> elements (line 1803), aria-live='polite' to training metrics region, role='status' to animation step counters. Add shape backup for color-only coding (scatter plot at line 1022, pipeline stages at line 372).
acceptanceCriteria: All mode tabs have role='tab' and aria-selected
All SVG canvases have role='img' and descriptive aria-label
Pipeline stages are keyboard-navigable with visible focus ring
Training metrics region has aria-live='polite'
Scatter plot uses shapes (circle vs triangle) in addition to color
axe-core audit finds zero critical violations

title: Replace all hardcoded hex colors with CSS custom properties
description: 100+ instances of hardcoded hex colors (#3b82f6, #f97316, #1e293b, #334155, #64748b, #94a3b8, etc.) across MLDesignModule.tsx and library files. Replace with CSS variables from globals.css: --foreground, --foreground-muted, --foreground-subtle, --primary, --border, --surface, --elevated, --accent. For SVG fills that need specific colors (dataset classes, pipeline stages), define semantic CSS vars like --ml-class-0, --ml-class-1, --ml-stage-ingestion, etc.
acceptanceCriteria: Zero hardcoded hex values in MLDesignModule.tsx (except in computed gradients)
Pipeline stage colors defined as CSS variables
All colors respond to theme changes
Visual appearance matches current design (no regressions)

title: Add tooltips explaining each sidebar control for beginners
description: Controls like Learning Rate, Activation, Epsilon, Hidden Layers, Neurons/Layer, Control Rate, Treatment Rate have no explanatory text. A beginner has no idea what these mean. Add tooltip components (hover or ? icon) with brief explanations. E.g., Learning Rate: 'Controls how much the network adjusts per training step. Higher = faster but less stable. Try 0.01-0.1.' Activation: 'The function applied to each neuron output. ReLU is most common; Sigmoid squashes to 0-1.'
acceptanceCriteria: Every sidebar control in all 6 modes has a tooltip with 1-2 sentence explanation
Tooltips appear on hover or ? icon click
Tooltips include a suggested value range where applicable
Tooltips do not interfere with slider interaction

title: Add stop/pause button for neural network training
description: Currently only Reset button exists (MLDesignModule.tsx:282-294) which clears all training results. Add a Pause button that sets cancelRef.current=true but preserves the trained network and metrics. Allow Resume to continue from the current epoch. Change Train button to Show Pause when training.
acceptanceCriteria: Pause button appears during training, replacing Train button
Pausing preserves current network weights, loss history, and decision boundary
Resume continues from the paused epoch
Reset still clears everything

title: Add bandit strategy comparison mode
description: Currently, changing bandit strategy (MLDesignModule.tsx:3430-3446) replaces previous results. Users cannot compare Epsilon-Greedy vs UCB1 vs Thompson side by side. Add a 'Compare All' button that runs all three strategies on the same arm configuration and shows results in a split view or overlaid chart.
acceptanceCriteria: Compare All button runs all 3 strategies with same arm count and rounds
Results shown side-by-side (3 bar charts or overlaid line chart)
Each strategy's final reward rate clearly labeled
Individual strategy mode still works as before

title: Move neural network training to Web Worker
description: Training at MLDesignModule.tsx:3532 runs synchronously on the main thread. For 1000 epochs on 200 samples with 4 layers of 8 neurons, each RAF frame blocks for the duration of forward+backward pass. Move training computation to a Web Worker using comlink (already installed). Post epoch metrics back for UI updates.
acceptanceCriteria: Training does not block the main thread (UI remains responsive during training)
Epoch metrics still update in real-time via postMessage/comlink
Decision boundary still updates during training
Cancel/Reset still works instantly

title: Consolidate duplicate NN and dataset implementations
description: Two neural network impls: neural-net.ts (simple, used) and neural-network.ts (advanced, orphaned). Two dataset impls: datasets.ts (simple, used) and dataset-generators.ts (rich, orphaned). Fix the optimizer bug in neural-network.ts (MLD-025), then migrate the main module to use neural-network.ts and dataset-generators.ts. Remove neural-net.ts and datasets.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: Only one NN implementation remains (neural-network.ts)
Only one dataset implementation remains (dataset-generators.ts)
Main module uses the consolidated implementations
All existing tests pass
neural-net.ts and datasets.ts deleted

title: Add pre-training concept explanations to each mode
description: No mode starts with an explanation of what the user will learn (Mayer's Principle 7). Add a brief 'What you'll learn' section at the top of each mode's sidebar, or a collapsible intro panel. NN mode: 'Train a neural network to classify 2D data. Watch the decision boundary form.' A/B Testing: 'Simulate statistical hypothesis testing. Learn when results are significant.' etc.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the 6 modes has a visible introductory explanation
Explanation is collapsible/dismissible
Explanation includes 1-2 key terms defined
First-time experience score improves from 6/10 to 8/10 in cognitive walkthrough

title: Add error boundary wrapping the module
description: No ErrorBoundary wraps any part of the module (MLDesignModule.tsx). An exception in ScatterPlot, NetworkDiagram, or any canvas component crashes the entire module with no recovery. Add React ErrorBoundary around each canvas section with a 'Something went wrong — click to retry' fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: ErrorBoundary wraps the canvas area
A thrown error in any visualization shows a friendly error UI, not a blank screen
User can click to retry without page refresh
Error details logged to console for debugging

title: Fix empty states to match app-wide icon+title pattern
description: Empty states at MLDesignModule.tsx:2397-2403 (A/B Testing), 2657-2663 (Bandit), 2971-2977 (Feature Store) use plain text. App standard (per AlgorithmModule.tsx:900-933) uses: h-16 w-16 icon with opacity-30 + text-lg title + text-sm description, centered. Update all empty states to match.
acceptanceCriteria: All empty state screens use the standard icon+title+description pattern
Icons match the mode (FlaskConical for A/B, Dices for Bandit, Database for FS)
Visual consistency with other modules' empty states

title: Custom-style all range inputs and select elements
description: 15 <input type='range'> elements use only accent-blue-500 with no custom track/thumb styling. 3 <select> elements use basic appearance-none. Replace with custom-styled components matching the app design system (per PropertiesPanel.tsx patterns). Use the existing Slider and Select UI components from @/components/ui/.
acceptanceCriteria: All range inputs use custom track and thumb styling matching app design
All select elements use the app's Select component from @/components/ui/select
Visual consistency with PropertiesPanel controls
Controls still work on mobile (touch-friendly)

title: Add prefers-reduced-motion support to all animations
description: Serving canvas animated dots (line 1926), Feature Store glowing nodes (line 3097), Bandit bar chart transitions, and NeuralNetViz SVG animations all play regardless of user's reduced motion preference. Add a CSS media query check or useReducedMotion hook. When enabled, show static snapshots instead of animations.
acceptanceCriteria: Animations pause when prefers-reduced-motion is enabled
Static snapshots show the final/current state instead of animating
SVG <animate> elements respect the preference
No layout shift when switching between animated and static modes

title: Fix O(n^2) rendering in CNN demo output grid
description: MLDesignModule.tsx:1373-1374 calls Math.max(...convResult.outputGrid.flat().map(v => Math.abs(v))) inside the render loop for every cell of the output grid. For a 6x6 grid, this creates 36 intermediate flattened arrays. Pre-compute maxAbs once before the map.
acceptanceCriteria: maxAbs is computed once per render, not per cell
CNN demo visual output is identical before and after fix
No new linting warnings

title: Add unit tests for neural-net.ts and untested library files
description: neural-net.ts (the NN used by main module) has zero tests. Also untested: datasets.ts, serving-patterns.ts, ab-testing.ts, multi-armed-bandit.ts, feature-store.ts, cnn-layer.ts, dropout-viz.ts, loss-landscape.ts. Add tests for: NN train convergence on XOR, z-test p-value against known values, UCB1 plays all arms once first, Thompson converges to best arm, feature store produces 8 steps.
acceptanceCriteria: neural-net.ts: tests for constructor, forward output range, train convergence on XOR
ab-testing.ts: z-test p-value matches known statistical table for controlRate=0.1, treatmentRate=0.15, n=5000
multi-armed-bandit.ts: UCB1 plays each arm once before exploiting, epsilon-greedy explores with correct probability
Test coverage for lib/ml-design/ exceeds 80%

title: Optimize training state updates to prevent excessive re-renders
description: MLDesignModule.tsx:3544-3545 calls setTrainingLog([...logAccumulator]) and setLossHistory([...lossAcc]) spreading the entire growing array every RAF frame. For 1000 epochs with 5 epochs/batch, this creates 200 intermediate copies of growing arrays. Batch state updates: only update every N epochs or use a ref for the log and update state less frequently.
acceptanceCriteria: Training log state updates at most once every 10 epochs (not every RAF frame)
Loss sparkline and training table still update smoothly
Memory usage during training remains flat (not growing linearly with epoch count)

title: Extract duplicate seededRandom into shared utility
description: seededRandom function is duplicated in 3 files: serving-patterns.ts:27, multi-armed-bandit.ts:31, feature-store.ts:39. Same implementation. Extract into a shared utils file.
acceptanceCriteria: Single seededRandom implementation in a shared utility file
All 3 consumer files import from the shared location
Simulation outputs are identical (same seeds produce same results)

title: Add CNN demo step descriptions that explain WHY
description: cnn-layer.ts:114 step description says 'Window at (0,0): element-wise multiply & sum = 1.23' — this says WHAT but not WHY. Enhance to explain the purpose: 'The filter slides across the input, computing a dot product at each position. High values indicate the filter pattern (e.g., edge) is present here. Result: 1.23 (strong match).'
acceptanceCriteria: Each CNN demo step description includes WHY the operation matters
Descriptions are beginner-friendly (no unexplained jargon)
Step descriptions for the first and last positions explain the overall convolution concept

title: Unify module naming: ML Playground everywhere
description: Module has 3 names: 'ML Design' in activity bar (activity-bar.tsx:44-58), 'ML Playground' in sidebar header (MLDesignModule.tsx:959), and 'ml-design' in code (ModuleRenderer.tsx:96-99 maps to 'ML Design'). Pick one user-facing name ('ML Playground') and use it consistently in all UI surfaces.
acceptanceCriteria: Activity bar shows 'ML Playground' as the module name
ModuleRenderer MODULE_NAMES maps ml-design to 'ML Playground'
Sidebar header says 'ML Playground'
Code-level identifiers remain 'ml-design' (no code rename needed)

title: Align active item highlighting to app standard
description: ML Design sidebar uses bg-accent text-foreground for active items (MLDesignModule.tsx:154,214). App standard per DataStructuresModule is bg-primary/10 text-primary font-medium. Update all button-group selectors (dataset, activation, serving mode, bandit strategy) to use the standard pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: All sidebar button selectors use bg-primary/10 text-primary font-medium for active state
Inactive state uses text-foreground-muted hover:bg-accent/50 hover:text-foreground
Visual matches DataStructuresModule sidebar pattern

title: Add legends to scatter plot, network diagram, and decision boundary
description: ScatterPlot (MLDesignModule.tsx:997-1029), NetworkDiagram (1038-1142), and DecisionBoundary (1151-1206) have no legends explaining what colors mean. Add: scatter plot legend showing blue=class 0, orange=class 1; network diagram legend for positive/negative weights; decision boundary color scale.
acceptanceCriteria: Scatter plot has a small legend showing class 0 and class 1 with their colors
Network diagram has legend for weight coloring (blue=positive, orange=negative)
Decision boundary has a gradient legend from class 0 to class 1
Legends don't overlap with the visualization

title: Add mode tabs responsive behavior for mobile
description: 6 mode tabs (MLDesignModule.tsx:964-980) at text-[9px] with flex-1 create tiny touch targets on mobile. On screens < 640px: switch to a horizontal scrollable row with larger labels, or a dropdown selector. Touch targets must be >= 44x44px.
acceptanceCriteria: Mode tabs have >= 44px touch target height on mobile viewports
All 6 mode labels are readable on 375px screen width
Mode switching works via touch on mobile
No horizontal overflow on the sidebar

title: Add pseudocode panel for NN backpropagation and bandit algorithms
description: No pseudocode is shown for any algorithm. Add a toggleable pseudocode panel (or bottom-panel tab) that shows the algorithm's pseudocode with the current step highlighted during animation. Priority: NN backpropagation, UCB1, Thompson Sampling.
acceptanceCriteria: NN mode has a viewable backpropagation pseudocode section
Bandit mode shows pseudocode for the selected strategy
Current execution step is highlighted in the pseudocode during animation
Pseudocode is syntax-highlighted and readable

title: Add learning path with suggested mode sequence and prerequisites
description: Content is organized for browsing not learning. Add a 'Learning Path' section accessible from the sidebar or a welcome screen that suggests: 1) Neural Network → 2) CNN → 3) Dropout → 4) A/B Testing → 5) Bandits → 6) Model Serving → 7) Feature Store. Each step shows prerequisites and what you'll learn.
acceptanceCriteria: Learning path is visible on first visit to the module
Each step shows: title, what you'll learn, prerequisites, estimated time
Clicking a step navigates to that mode
Path can be dismissed but accessible via a sidebar button

title: Add Transformer / Attention visualization mode
description: Every 2026 ML syllabus (Stanford CS229, MIT 6.390), FAANG interview guide, and competitor analysis lists Transformers as essential. ML Visualized and Transformer Explainer prove in-browser attention visualization is feasible. Add a new mode showing: token embeddings, self-attention matrix heatmap, multi-head attention, positional encoding, and feedforward layers with step-by-step animation.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Transformer' mode accessible from mode tabs
Self-attention weight matrix shown as interactive heatmap
Step-by-step animation through: embed → attention → feedforward → output
User can input a short sentence and see token attention patterns
Sidebar explains each component with beginner-friendly text

title: Add Linear and Logistic Regression interactive mode
description: Linear/Logistic Regression is the #1 foundational ML topic in every syllabus and interview. Add a mode with: interactive data point placement, regression line fitting with animated gradient descent, cost function visualization, decision boundary for logistic regression, adjustable learning rate showing convergence speed.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Regression' mode with linear and logistic sub-modes
User can click to add data points on a 2D canvas
Regression line/curve updates in real-time during gradient descent
Cost function shown as a curve with current position marked
Learning rate slider shows effect on convergence (too high = diverges, too low = slow)

title: Add K-Means clustering interactive visualization
description: K-Means is universally taught and asked in interviews. Add a mode with: random/click-to-place data points, step-by-step animation showing centroid movement, Voronoi cell boundaries, elbow method chart for choosing K. Users should see each iteration: assign points → update centroids → repeat.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Clustering' mode or sub-mode accessible from the module
Step-by-step animation: assign → update centroids with visible movement
Voronoi-style boundaries shown around clusters
K slider (2-8) with elbow method plot in properties panel
User can click to add custom data points

title: Add Decision Tree / Random Forest visualization
description: Decision Trees are essential for ML interviews (explain splitting criteria, impurity measures, pruning). Add a mode showing: interactive tree building on 2D data, Gini/entropy split visualization, decision boundary overlay, Random Forest as ensemble of trees with majority vote animation.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Decision Tree' mode with interactive tree visualization
Each split shows the feature threshold and impurity measure
Decision boundary updates as tree grows
Random Forest mode shows multiple trees and their vote aggregation
Properties panel explains Gini vs Entropy trade-off

title: Add Gradient Descent variants comparison visualization
description: Integrate the orphaned LossLandscapeCanvas.tsx to show a 3D loss surface with animated optimizer paths. Let users run SGD, Momentum, Adam, RMSProp simultaneously on the same surface and watch them converge differently. This is the #1 'share-worthy' visualization missing from the module.
acceptanceCriteria: 3D loss landscape renders using LossLandscapeCanvas.tsx (currently orphaned)
User can select 2-4 optimizers to run simultaneously
Each optimizer's path shown as a colored trail on the surface
Speed control (slow/normal/fast) for animation
Sidebar explains each optimizer's update rule

title: Add PCA / t-SNE dimensionality reduction visualization
description: PCA and t-SNE are asked in every ML interview and taught in every syllabus. Add a mode showing: high-dimensional data projected to 2D/3D, interactive rotation of projected space, explained variance plot for PCA, perplexity parameter for t-SNE, animated convergence of t-SNE iterations.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Dimensionality Reduction' mode with PCA and t-SNE sub-modes
PCA shows eigenvectors overlaid on data, explained variance bar chart
t-SNE shows animated convergence with perplexity slider
Pre-loaded datasets (iris, digits, blobs) available for selection
Sidebar explains variance preservation vs local structure preservation

title: Add Overfitting / Underfitting interactive demonstration
description: Bias-variance tradeoff is a CRITICAL gap (D20). Add a visualization showing: train vs validation loss curves diverging, model complexity slider (polynomial degree or layer count), clear visual of underfitting (high bias), overfitting (high variance), and the sweet spot. This is the most-searched concept on r/learnprogramming for ML.
acceptanceCriteria: Train and validation loss curves shown simultaneously
Model complexity slider that demonstrates underfitting → good fit → overfitting
Visual label indicating current regime (underfitting/good/overfitting)
Regularization slider (L2) showing its effect on overfitting
Educational callout explaining bias-variance tradeoff

title: Add Confusion Matrix / ROC / AUC evaluation metrics panel
description: ML interview prep requires understanding classification metrics. After training the NN, show: confusion matrix heatmap, precision/recall/F1 per class, ROC curve with AUC, threshold slider showing precision-recall tradeoff. Currently the module only shows accuracy and loss.
acceptanceCriteria: Confusion matrix rendered as a color-coded 2x2 grid after NN training
Precision, recall, F1 computed and displayed
ROC curve plotted with AUC value
Threshold slider that updates all metrics in real-time
Properties panel or bottom panel tab shows these metrics

title: Add progress tracking and completion system
description: D23 Octalysis audit scored 0/5 on Accomplishment drive. Add: mode completion badges (train a network = badge), progress bar across all modes ('3/8 explored'), concept mastery indicators. Store in localStorage. Show on module entry as motivation.
acceptanceCriteria: Each mode has 1-3 milestone achievements (e.g., 'Train to 90% accuracy on Spiral')
Overall progress bar visible in sidebar header ('3/8 modes explored')
Completed milestones shown as badges/checkmarks per mode tab
Progress persists across page refreshes via localStorage
Welcome screen shows progress summary on return visits

title: Add interactive challenges per mode
description: D23 scored 0/5 on Scarcity drive — no goals or challenges exist. Add per-mode challenges: NN mode ('Achieve 95% on Spiral with ≤2 layers'), Bandit mode ('Find the best arm in ≤50 rounds'), A/B Testing ('Detect a 2% lift with smallest sample size'). Show challenge as a banner, celebrate completion.
acceptanceCriteria: Each mode has at least 2 challenges of varying difficulty
Active challenge shown as a banner above the canvas
Challenge completion triggers a celebration animation (confetti or glow)
Completed challenges persist in localStorage
Challenges teach an important concept (not just arbitrary numbers)

title: Add 'Predict then Reveal' interaction to NN training
description: D21 found zero germane load — passive watching is the primary mode. Add a 'Predict Mode' where the user sees untrained data and draws their own decision boundary guess before training. After training, overlay the actual boundary to compare. This transforms passive watching into active learning (Mayer's engagement principle).
acceptanceCriteria: Before training, user can draw a freehand boundary on the canvas
After training, both user's guess and actual boundary are shown overlaid
Similarity score between user guess and actual boundary displayed
Option to skip prediction and train directly
Works for all 4 dataset types

title: Add algorithm sonification — hear the difference between optimizers
description: Phase 4 'first in the world' idea: Map training loss to audio pitch so users can HEAR convergence. Fast descent = falling pitch. Oscillation = warbling. Plateau = sustained tone. Different optimizers produce different 'melodies'. Uses Web Audio API for zero-latency synthesis. This would be the first algorithm visualizer where you can hear O(n²) vs O(n log n) convergence.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle button enables audio sonification during NN training
Loss value mapped to pitch (high loss = high pitch, low = low)
Audible difference between fast convergence and slow convergence
Mute/volume control available
Works in all browsers with Web Audio API support

title: Add one-click export of training animation as video/GIF
description: Phase 4 research found content creators need shareable content. Add a 'Record' button that captures the training animation using MediaRecorder API and exports as WebM/GIF. This makes every training run a potential YouTube thumbnail or Twitter post.
acceptanceCriteria: Record button appears in canvas toolbar
Recording captures the decision boundary evolution during training
Export as WebM video or animated GIF
Recording includes a watermark/attribution to Architex
File size reasonable (<5MB for 30s recording)

title: Add multiplayer algorithm race via WebRTC
description: Phase 4 'first in the world' idea: Two users race to achieve target accuracy on the same dataset with different hyperparameters. Real-time split screen showing both decision boundaries forming. Uses WebRTC for peer-to-peer sync. Inspired by Kahoot's competitive learning model.
acceptanceCriteria: Share link generates a room code for 2 players
Both players see the same dataset, choose their own hyperparameters
Split-screen shows both decision boundaries training simultaneously
First to reach target accuracy wins
Works without a backend server (WebRTC peer-to-peer)

title: Fix spatial contiguity: move training metrics next to decision boundary
description: D22 Mayer's Principle 2 scored 2/5: training metrics are in the properties panel (right side) while the decision boundary is in the canvas (center-right). User must look 200+ px away to correlate metrics with visual changes. Move key metrics (epoch, loss, accuracy) as an overlay on the canvas near the decision boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Epoch, loss, and accuracy shown as a small overlay on the decision boundary canvas
Overlay positioned at top-left or bottom-left of the boundary viz, not in a separate panel
Properties panel still shows detailed metrics for users who want them
Overlay is semi-transparent and doesn't obscure the visualization

title: Add guided onboarding tour for first-time visitors
description: D16 cognitive walkthrough scored 6/10 — no guidance for first-time users. Add a dismissible onboarding overlay that highlights: 1) dataset selector, 2) Train button, 3) decision boundary canvas, 4) mode tabs. Use a step-by-step spotlight pattern. Show only on first visit (localStorage flag).
acceptanceCriteria: First visit shows a 4-step guided tour with spotlight highlights
Tour dismissable at any step with 'Skip Tour' button
Tour not shown on subsequent visits (localStorage flag)
Tour re-accessible via a '?' button in sidebar header
Each step has 1 sentence explaining what to do

title: Add keyboard shortcuts for common actions
description: D3 Nielsen's Flexibility heuristic rated Poor — no keyboard shortcuts exist. Add: Space = Train/Pause, R = Reset, 1-6 = switch modes, Left/Right = step through animations, Escape = close expanded panels. Register with ui-store shortcuts dialog.
acceptanceCriteria: Space bar starts/pauses training in NN mode
R key resets the current mode
Number keys 1-6 switch between modes
Left/Right arrows step through animations (serving, bandit, feature store)
Shortcuts shown in the app's shortcuts dialog (ui-store integration)
Shortcuts don't fire when typing in input fields

title: Add ARIA live regions for dynamic training/animation state
description: Granular breakdown of MLD-029 (accessibility). Training metrics, animation step counters, and simulation results update dynamically but are invisible to screen readers. Add aria-live='polite' to: training epoch/loss/accuracy display (NNProperties:2041-2060), serving step counter (ServingProperties:2193), bandit round counter (BanditProperties:2811), feature store step counter (FeatureStoreProperties:3194).
acceptanceCriteria: Training metrics container has aria-live='polite'
Screen reader announces epoch milestones (every 10th epoch)
Animation step changes announced for serving/bandit/feature-store modes
Announcements are throttled to avoid overwhelming the screen reader

title: Add keyboard navigation to SVG interactive elements
description: Granular breakdown of MLD-029 (accessibility). Pipeline stage <g> elements (MLDesignModule.tsx:1803), serving model boxes, and feature store nodes respond to onClick but have no keyboard support. Add tabIndex={0}, onKeyDown (Enter/Space to select), role='button', aria-label with stage/node name, visible focus ring.
acceptanceCriteria: Pipeline stages are focusable via Tab key
Enter/Space selects a focused pipeline stage
Focused stage has a visible focus ring (2px blue outline)
Each stage has aria-label with its name and type
Same pattern applied to serving model boxes and feature store nodes

title: Add shape differentiation backup for color-coded elements
description: Granular breakdown of MLD-029 (accessibility). Scatter plot data points (MLDesignModule.tsx:1017-1024) use only color (blue=class 0, orange=class 1). Pipeline stages (line 372) use color-coded dots. Network layers (line 1112-1116) are color-only. Add shape backup: class 0 = circle, class 1 = triangle/square. Pipeline types = different shapes (circle, diamond, hexagon).
acceptanceCriteria: Scatter plot class 0 uses circles, class 1 uses triangles
Pipeline stage type indicators use distinct shapes per type
Network diagram input/hidden/output layers have distinct shapes
All visualizations are interpretable in grayscale
Legend updated to show both color and shape

title: Add Reinforcement Learning grid-world sandbox
description: RL (MDPs, Q-Learning, Policy Gradient) is covered in CS229 and increasingly asked in FAANG interviews. Add a grid-world mode where: user draws walls/rewards, agent explores using Q-learning or SARSA, Q-values shown as cell colors, policy arrows shown per cell, step-by-step or auto-play animation.
acceptanceCriteria: Grid world with editable walls, start, and goal positions
Q-learning agent explores and learns, Q-values visualized as cell colors
Optimal policy shown as arrows in each cell
Step-by-step mode with exploration/exploitation highlighted
Epsilon-greedy exploration parameter adjustable via slider

title: Add 'Why' explanations to serving pattern step descriptions
description: D6 found serving pattern steps say WHAT but not WHY. serving-patterns.ts:88-92 says 'Collecting data: 1000 requests processed' without explaining why data collection is needed before significance testing. Enhance all step descriptions across serving, bandit, and feature store to include the WHY.
acceptanceCriteria: Every serving pattern step description includes a WHY clause
Example: 'Collecting data: 1000 requests processed. We need enough samples to detect a statistically significant difference between models.'
Bandit step descriptions explain exploration vs exploitation reasoning
Feature store steps already have good WHY explanations — verify, don't regress

title: Add state persistence via localStorage for training results
description: D23 Ownership scored 0/5 — page refresh destroys everything. Save: trained network weights, training metrics, current mode, hyperparameters, completed challenges. Restore on page load. Include a manual 'Save Snapshot' / 'Load Snapshot' for named configurations.
acceptanceCriteria: Trained network weights saved to localStorage on training completion
Current mode and hyperparameters restored on page load
Training metrics (loss history, accuracy) restored from last session
Manual 'Save' and 'Load' buttons in sidebar for named snapshots
Graceful handling of corrupted localStorage (clear and start fresh)

title: Add share results button with URL encoding
description: D23 Social scored 0/5 — no sharing mechanism. Add a 'Share' button that encodes the current configuration (dataset type, layers, neurons, activation, learning rate, epochs) into URL query parameters. Opening the URL reconstructs the same setup. Include an 'Export as Image' option for the canvas.
acceptanceCriteria: Share button generates a URL with encoded hyperparameters
Opening the shared URL restores the exact configuration
Copy-to-clipboard with success toast
Export as PNG button captures the canvas area
Shared URLs work without authentication

title: Add Train/Val split toggle showing overfitting in real-time
description: Connected to MLD-058 (overfitting demo). Even without a full new mode, add a simple toggle in NN mode that splits the dataset 80/20 and shows both train and validation loss curves. When the curves diverge, highlight with a warning label 'Overfitting detected'. This addresses the #1 curriculum gap with minimal effort.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle in NN sidebar to enable train/val split
When enabled, 20% of data held out for validation
Both train and validation loss shown in sparkline and bottom panel
Visual warning when val loss increases while train loss decreases
Tooltip explains what overfitting means

title: Add component tests for MLDesignModule rendering
description: No component tests exist for any of the 25+ React components in MLDesignModule.tsx. Add: render test for each mode (no crash), training start/stop lifecycle, mode switching preserves state, empty state rendering. Use vitest + @testing-library/react.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the 6 modes renders without crashing
Training starts, updates metrics, and can be reset
Switching modes and switching back preserves NN training state
Empty states render correctly for A/B Testing, Bandit, Feature Store
At least 15 test cases covering the golden paths

title: Add auto-train-on-load for immediate first impression
description: D25 HEART Adoption: time to first aha is ~15s (user must manually click Train). Reduce to <5s by auto-running a short training (20 epochs) on the Circle dataset when NN mode first loads. Show the decision boundary forming immediately. User can then customize and retrain.
acceptanceCriteria: On first load of NN mode, a 20-epoch training auto-runs
Decision boundary visibly forms within 2 seconds of mode appearing
Auto-train uses sensible defaults (Circle, 2 layers, 4 neurons, sigmoid, lr=0.1)
User can interrupt with Reset and configure their own parameters
Auto-train does NOT run on subsequent mode visits in the same session

title: Add SVM with kernel trick visualization
description: SVMs with kernels are a FAANG interview staple. Add a mode showing: linear SVM with margin and support vectors highlighted, kernel trick lifting data to higher dimensions (animated 2D→3D projection), RBF/polynomial kernel comparison on non-linear data, C parameter (regularization) slider.
acceptanceCriteria: SVM mode shows decision boundary with margin lines
Support vectors highlighted with distinct markers
Kernel trick animated: 2D data lifts to 3D, hyperplane separates, projects back
C parameter slider shows effect on margin width
RBF and polynomial kernel comparison on same dataset

title: Add CNN architecture builder with drag-and-drop layers
description: Wire the orphaned CNNVisualizer.tsx into a mode where users can drag-and-drop CNN layers (Conv2D, MaxPool, Flatten, Dense) to build architectures. Show dimension changes in real-time using the existing cnn-forward.ts simulator. Include preset architectures (LeNet, VGG, Tiny) as starting points.
acceptanceCriteria: Drag-and-drop interface for adding/removing/reordering CNN layers
Real-time dimension calculation as layers are added (via cnn-forward.ts)
3D isometric visualization from CNNVisualizer.tsx updates live
Parameter count and memory estimate shown in properties panel
Preset architectures loadable as starting points

title: Add Batch Normalization and L1/L2 regularization demos
description: D20 found regularization coverage is partial (only dropout exists). Add: L1/L2 weight penalty visualization showing weight distribution narrowing, Batch Normalization visualization showing activation distribution before/after normalization per layer. These are interview topics and extend the existing NN mode.
acceptanceCriteria: L2 regularization toggle in NN sidebar with lambda slider
Weight distribution histogram shown in properties panel (before/after regularization)
Batch normalization toggle showing activation distribution per layer
Educational text explaining why regularization helps prevent overfitting
Visual comparison: same network with and without regularization

title: Add 'Make it Fail' challenge mode — find worst-case inputs
description: Phase 4 inversion technique: instead of 'make the algorithm succeed', challenge users to 'make it FAIL'. E.g., 'Find hyperparameters where the NN achieves <50% accuracy on Circle' or 'Find a dataset where 1000 epochs of training doesn't converge'. This teaches failure modes and edge cases — crucial for interviews.
acceptanceCriteria: Challenge mode accessible via toggle in each applicable mode
At least 3 'Make it Fail' challenges across NN and bandit modes
Success = finding parameters that cause the specified failure
Educational explanation of WHY the failure occurs after completion
Challenges teach real concepts: vanishing gradients, learning rate explosion, explore-exploit imbalance

title: Add embeddable widget mode for blog/article integration
description: GitHub research found algorithm-visualizer issue #278 (embeddable version) with 5+ comments of demand. Distill.pub's model proves interactive inline visualizations drive engagement. Add an iframe-embeddable mode with URL parameters for configuration, minimal chrome, and a 'Powered by Architex' attribution link.
acceptanceCriteria: URL parameter ?embed=true renders the module without the app shell
Configuration via URL params: mode, dataset, layers, activation
Minimal chrome: no mode tabs unless specified, no sidebar unless specified
Attribution link 'Built with Architex' at bottom
Responsive to iframe container size
